title: ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN may corrupt data
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(a INT PRIMARY KEY, b VARCHAR(255), c FLOAT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0);
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c) VALUES (1, 'test', 1.1), (2, NULL, 2.2), (3, 'example', NULL);
CREATE TABLE t3(x INT, y INT, FOREIGN KEY(x) REFERENCES t2(a));
INSERT INTO t3(x, y) SELECT a, c FROM t2 WHERE b IS NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE t2 DROP COLUMN b;
key_bug_features: ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN b affects data integrity
